title: Inter-Token Latency
description: The serving latency metric that measures the delay between streamed output tokens after generation has started.
openingSummary: Inter-token latency is the delay between streamed output tokens after generation has started: each gap measures how quickly the serving system emits the next token once streaming is underway.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: Inter-token latency is the elapsed time between consecutive streamed output tokens after generation has started.

whyItMatters.title: Why It Matters
whyItMatters.body: Readers feel inter-token latency as the rhythm of a streaming reply after the first token appears.

simpleExample.title: Simple Example
simpleExample.body: Once a chat answer begins streaming, each gap between visible tokens is one inter-token latency interval.

commonConfusions.title: Common Confusions
commonConfusions.body: Inter-token latency is not time to first token or tokens per second.
